knownIssues.title: DPVR Assistant software is less mature than Oculus/SteamVR tooling
knownIssues.description: The DPVR ecosystem is smaller; occasional driver bugs and slower response on new SteamVR updates are common. Community support is limited.
knownIssues.workaround: Keep DPVR Assistant updated. Check DPVR's support forum for known bugs. SteamVR compatibility is improving but still trails other vendors by 1-2 versions.

knownIssues.title: Inside-out tracking quality is below Quest/Pico level
knownIssues.description: The E4's tracking cameras cover a narrower volume than Meta Quest or Pico devices. Controllers behind the back or at full extension can lose tracking.
knownIssues.workaround: Keep controllers in front of the headset cameras as much as possible. Archery/shooter games with weapon behind back motions are affected most.

optimizationTips.tip: Budget-class pricing — match supersampling to your GPU tier
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: The E4 targets affordable VR. Don't push supersampling above 1.0× on mid-range GPUs — you'll get more benefit from in-app quality settings.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: At 2160×2160 native per eye and 1.0× SteamVR supersampling, a GTX 1060 can hold 90 FPS in most non-AAA titles. Leave supersampling at 1.0× and prioritize MSAA / in-app AA settings.
